calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-01-2025 Set For Trial On Sep-14-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND XIAO HUANG's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The City and County of San Francisco demurs to plaintiff Josie Acosta's second cause of action for negligence per se, as pleaded in her First Amended Complaint (FAC), on grounds that it does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action.

The negligence per se doctrine . . . creates a presumption of negligence if four elements are established: (1) the defendant violated a statute, ordinance, or regulation of a public entity; (2) the violation proximately caused death or injury to person or property; (3) the death or injury resulted from an occurrence of the nature of which the statute, ordinance, or regulation was designed to prevent; and (4) the person suffering the death or the injury to his person or property was one of the class of persons for whose protection the statute, ordinance, or regulation was adopted. The first two elements are questions of fact, while the latter two are questions of law." (Spates v. Dameron Hospital Assn. (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 208, 218 [internal quotation marks and citation omitted].)

The court overrules the demurrer. Acosta alleges that she is a disabled person who tripped over a ramp that should not have been extended while she was trying to board a bus. (FAC para. 11.) She alleges the bus driver failed to provide required assistance to her as a disabled passenger, which resulted in her fall. (FAC para. 16.) She alleges that defendants violated Civil Code sections protecting disabled people and that she was in the class of persons protected by those laws. (FAC para. 30.) These allegations on their face satisfy the factual elements of negligence per se.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-03-2025 Continued To Jul-16-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
